signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives inflation surge, pushing Fed rate cuts to late 2026 — Reuters poll. This macro backdrop supports USD. USD/JPY at 159.5, testing the BoJ intervention watch level of 160.00. The 450bp Fed-BoJ carry differential favors USD. Risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160.00.
evidence: Fed rate cuts pushed to late 2026

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +41,675 (bullish spec positioning) vs retail 89% short (extreme retail short). HV30 at 6.8%, ATM IV at 8.2% — options pricing a 1.4 vol-pt premium over realized. The combination of net long positioning and extreme retail short divergence signals late-trend caution. A LF delta turning positive or retail reducing shorts would invalidate the caution read.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Yield
signals.text: BoE 3.75% vs Fed 3.75% — 0bp spread; structural carry advantage neutral. GBP LF net long +28,426; AM constructive. GBP/USD at 1.3496, mid-range; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation. ATM IV 9.5% near HV; options neutral.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT + OIS
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short −17,090 (specs short) vs RBNZ OIS hike 100% priced. Specs are fading a hawkish CB. If RBNZ delivers or signals hike, short squeeze risk is elevated. NZD/USD at 0.5901; positioning aligned with CB bias.
